entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The agricultural crop whose price fluctuations are analyzed for their impact on socio-economic factors.

entities.name: RICE PRICES
entities.description: The rising cost of rice in international and local markets.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of lacking sufficient financial resources, measured by the poverty rate.

entities.name: INCOME INEQUALITY
entities.description: The extent to which income is distributed unevenly among a population.

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: The West African country where the impact of rice prices was assessed.

entities.name: COMPENSATING VARIATION
entities.description: A methodology used to measure the change in welfare resulting from a price change.

entities.name: NET BENEFIT RATIO
entities.description: A specific ratio developed by Deaton to analyze the impact of price changes on welfare.

entities.name: LIVING STANDARD SURVEY
entities.description: A survey tool used to collect data on household living standards and consumption.

entities.name: URBAN AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by city environments where poverty and inequality increases were more pronounced.

entities.name: RURAL AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by countryside environments where poverty increases were observed.

relationships.description: Rising rice prices increase the poverty rate, especially for net buyers.
Rising rice prices lead to an increase in income inequality.
The study assesses the impact of rising rice prices specifically within Burkina Faso.
Used as a methodological basis to assess the impact of price changes on poverty.
Used as a methodological basis to assess the impact of price changes on poverty.
Provides the empirical data used to calculate the poverty rate.
The increase in the poverty rate is higher in urban areas than in rural areas.
Income inequality increases particularly in urban areas.
The price of rice is the specific condition being studied.
